blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Surgical Treatment of the Fetus?
Fetal surgery วิวัฒนาการจากแนวคิดสู่ clinical standard ใน 4 ทศวรรษ. สิ่งที่ neonatologist ต้องรู้: (1) MOMS trial (Adzick NEJM 2011): open fetal MMC repair → ↓ shunt ต้องการที่ 12M (40% vs 82%) + motor improvement; (2) TTTS laser ablation (Senat NEJM 2004): fetoscopic laser > amniodrainage — survival ≥1 twin 76% vs 56%; (3) FETO/TOTAL trial: tracheal occlusion — benefit ใน severe isolated left CDH (40% vs 15%) แต่ NOT moderate; (4) EXIT procedure: neonatologist ต้อง scrub + present in OR; (5) Betamethasone → regression ใน 75% ของ microcystic CPAM with hydrops; (6) Maternal risks ของ open surgery: preterm 79%, mandatory CS future.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย milestones: Harrison 1981 (uropathy), Senat 2004 (TTTS), Adzick 2011 (MMC), Deprest 2019/2021 (CDH FETO)
แยกประเภท intervention: open hysterotomy, fetoscopic/minimally invasive, US-guided percutaneous
รู้ MOMS trial: criteria, primary outcome (shunt 40% vs 82%), maternal tradeoffs
อธิบาย Quintero I–V TTTS staging + Eurofetus trial + Solomon technique
อธิบาย CDH severity (o/e LHR) + FETO/TOTAL: severe benefit, moderate no benefit
รู้ EXIT procedure: indications (CHAOS, neck mass, FETO balloon) + neonatologist checklist
อธิบาย LUTO/vesicoamniotic shunting + PLUTO trial + favorable urine markers
รู้ CPAM CVR >1.6 + betamethasone for microcystic + shunt for macrocystic
อธิบาย fetal anesthesia: transplacental GA + direct IM (fentanyl/vecuronium/atropine)
ระบุ maternal risks + ethical principles (equipoise, maternal autonomy, center of excellence)
1. Historical Development of Fetal Surgery
Fetal surgery เริ่มจากแนวคิดว่า fetal conditions บางอย่าง progressive + lethal แต่ correctable ก่อน damage ถาวร. Michael Harrison, UCSF คือ pioneer.

rows: Harrison — first open fetal surgery (obstructive uropathy)
UCSF; vesicostomy in fetus; proof of concept สำหรับ open fetal surgery
TTTS treatment, LUTO shunting, cardiac interventions เริ่ม feasible
Senat et al. NEJM — Eurofetus RCT (TTTS)
Fetoscopic laser > amniodrainage: survival ≥1 twin 76% vs 56%; first major RCT in fetal intervention
Adzick et al. NEJM — MOMS trial (MMC)
Open prenatal MMC repair: ↓ shunt 40% vs 82%; stopped early for benefit; center of excellence required
Solomon et al. Lancet — Solomon technique (TTTS)
Equatorial + selective coagulation: ↓ TTTS recurrence + ↓ TAPS; now preferred
Deprest et al. Lancet — TOTAL trial (FETO for CDH)
Severe CDH: FETO 40% vs 15%. Moderate: no benefit + ↑ PPROM
Fetoscopic MMC (MOMS2), in utero gene therapy

blocks.text: 2. Types of Fetal Intervention
2.1 Open Fetal Surgery (Hysterotomy)
Maternal laparotomy + stapled hysterotomy; fetus partially exteriorized; uterus closed; สำหรับ complex repairs ที่ทำ fetoscopically ไม่ได้
Indications: MMC repair (MOMS criteria), giant SCT with hydrops, CPAM/CCAM refractory to steroids with mirror syndrome
Maternal costs: blood loss, uterine scar (mandatory CS all future pregnancies), pulmonary edema, PPROM 30–50%, preterm labor; window: 19–26 weeks
2.2 Fetoscopic / Minimally Invasive
1–5 mm ports; less maternal morbidity; limited to procedures feasible through small working channel
Indications: TTTS (laser), FETO (CDH balloon), thoracoamniotic/vesicoamniotic shunting, fetoscopic MMC repair (emerging)
Risks: PPROM 10–30%, preterm labor, placental abruption, co-twin demise
2.3 Ultrasound-Guided Percutaneous
Needle/catheter under US — least invasive; outpatient feasible; includes amniocentesis, cordocentesis, IUT, double-pigtail shunting, intravascular therapy
3. Myelomeningocele (MMC) — MOMS Trial
3.1 Rationale

Exposed cord → 2 mechanisms of damage: (1) direct trauma + amniotic fluid toxicity; (2) Chiari II hindbrain herniation → obstructive hydrocephalus. Prenatal repair closes lesion before cumulative damage + reverses Chiari II in many cases → ↓ shunt need.
3.2 MOMS Trial (Adzick NS et al. NEJM 2011;364:993)

MOMS Trial — Criteria + Outcomes ท่องจำ
Eligibility: singleton, MMC T1–S1, GA 19–25+6 wk, no chromosomal anomaly, BMI <35, no prior preterm <37 wk, no uterine anomaly. Primary: death or VP shunt by 12M — prenatal 40% vs postnatal 82% (RR 0.48, p<0.001). Secondary: motor function better than lesion predicts by ~2 grades; Chiari II reversal in many. Maternal tradeoffs: 79% born <37 wk (vs 15%), PPROM 46%, mandatory CS all future pregnancies, pulmonary edema. Trial stopped early for benefit.
3.3 Fetoscopic MMC Repair
Developed to ↓ PPROM + preterm vs open while preserving benefit; MOMS2 trial (NICHD, ongoing): fetoscopic vs open — awaited
Lapa Neto et al. Lancet 2018: comparable neurological outcomes at high-volume centers; PPROM still ~45%
Neonatologist post-MMC: expect PPROM + preterm; shunt still needed in 40%; head US q2 wk; Chiari II → assess swallowing/vocal cord/lateral gaze; consults: neurosurgery, urology, ortho, ophthalmology
4. Twin-to-Twin Transfusion Syndrome (TTTS)
4.1 Pathophysiology + Quintero Staging

MC diamniotic twins: unbalanced inter-twin placental anastomoses → donor: hypovolemia + oligohydramnios; recipient: hypervolemia + polyhydramnios + cardiac overload. Untreated severe TTTS: mortality >80%.

rows: Donor: oligohydramnios (MVP <2 cm); Recipient: polyhydramnios (MVP >8 cm). Donor bladder VISIBLE
Not visualized on 60-min scan; Doppler normal bilaterally
Donor: AEDF/REDF umbilical artery; Recipient: reversed a-wave DV or pulsatile umbilical vein
Hydrops (skin edema, ascites, pleural/pericardial effusion) in ≥1 twin

blocks.text: 4.2 Eurofetus RCT + Solomon Technique

Eurofetus (Senat MV et al. NEJM 2004;351:136) — Standard of Care
TTTS Quintero II–IV at 15–26 wk: laser vs serial amniodrainage. Laser: survival ≥1 twin 76% vs 56% (p=0.009); neurological morbidity at 6M: 31% laser vs 52% drainage (fewer neuro complications with laser). Fetoscopic laser = selective photocoagulation of all inter-twin anastomoses.
Solomon technique (Lancet 2014): selective + complete equatorial line across inter-twin membrane → ↓ recurrent TTTS (1% vs 7%) + ↓ TAPS (3% vs 16%); Solomon = preferred technique
Post-laser: PPROM ~25–30%; monitor TAPS (MCA PSV: donor >1.5 MoM anemia, recipient <1.0 MoM polycythemia)
Neonatologist: donor = anemia + IUGR + renal tubular injury; recipient = polycythemia (partial exchange if Hct >65%) + cardiomegaly + TR + PPHN risk
5. Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia (CDH) — FETO / TOTAL Trial
5.1 Rationale + CDH Severity

Lung hypoplasia + PPHN = primary death cause. FETO: prevent fetal lung fluid efflux → ↑ lung stretch → ↑ lung growth + surfactant production. Balloon inserted fetoscopically at 27–29 wk (severe) → removed at 34 wk or at EXIT.

rows: FETO at specialized center (TOTAL: benefit confirmed)

blocks.text: 5.2 TOTAL Trial

TOTAL Trial (Deprest J et al. Lancet 2019 + 2021) — รู้ก่อน counseling ครอบครัว CDH
Severe CDH (Lancet 2021): FETO vs expectant — survival 40% vs 15% (p=0.008); significant benefit. Moderate CDH (Lancet 2019): survival 54% vs 65% — NO benefit; PPROM 47% FETO vs 11%. Conclusion: FETO only for severe isolated left CDH at specialized center. DO NOT offer for moderate CDH outside of clinical trial.
Post-FETO birth: NO bag-mask → immediate intubation + NGT; iNO ± ECMO; postnatal CDH repair after hemodynamic stabilization (days to weeks)
6. EXIT Procedure — Ex Utero Intrapartum Treatment
6.1 Concept + Steps
EXIT = partial delivery บน placental circulation → 30–60 min window ของ airway securing ก่อน cord clamp. Requires: deep halogenated GA (uterine relaxation) + preserved uteroplacental gas exchange.

rows: Laryngeal atresia, tracheal agenesis, subglottic stenosis
Secure airway before mass occludes trachea
Controlled balloon removal before airway secured
Giant intrathoracic lesion or SCT with cardiac failure

blocks.text: Steps: (1) Maternal deep halogenated GA; (2) hysterotomy; (3) partial delivery (head + neck); (4) airway procedure (bronchoscopy/tracheostomy); (5) fetal monitoring (SpO2 + US cardiac); (6) cord clamp only after airway confirmed

CHAOS — Neonatologist Must Be Scrubbed In the OR
CHAOS: laryngeal/tracheal atresia → normal intubation impossible → death ภายในนาทีแรก. Neonatologist checklist: scrubbed + in OR BEFORE procedure; bronchoscopes 2.5+3.0 mm; paediatric surgeon scrubbed; tracheostomy tray open; surfactant drawn; ECMO notified; cord clamp ONLY after CO2 confirmed.
7. Lower Urinary Tract Obstruction (LUTO) — Vesicoamniotic Shunting
Causes: posterior urethral valves (PUV, ~63%), urethral atresia, prune belly. Obstruction → bladder distension → hydronephrosis → renal dysplasia + oligohydramnios → pulmonary hypoplasia.
Technique: Harrison double-pigtail bladder catheter (percutaneous US-guided): proximal coil in fetal bladder + distal in amniotic cavity → drain urine → ↑ AF

PLUTO Trial (Morris RK et al. Lancet 2013;382:1496) — Underpowered
Enrolled 31/150 planned; shunt 50% vs 27% survival — not significant (p=0.25). No definitive RCT evidence. Patient selection — favorable fetal urine (serial aspirations q48–72h): Na <100 mEq/L + Cl <90 mEq/L + Osm <210 mOsm/L + β2-microglobulin <4 mg/L → preserved renal tubular function → ↑ chance of benefit.

Neonatologist: renal function: Cr at day 2–3 (day 1 = maternal); pulmonary hypoplasia ขึ้นกับ oligohydramnios duration; cystoscopy + PUV ablation electively; long-term bladder dysfunction + CKD monitoring
8. Congenital Pulmonary Airway Malformation (CPAM)
CVR = (L × H × W × 0.523) / head circumference (Crombleholme Radiology 2002); CVR >1.6 → 75% hydrops risk; CVR <1.6 → <3%
Microcystic CPAM with hydrops: maternal betamethasone 12 mg IM × 2 doses q24h → regression in ~75% (Peranteau J Pediatr Surg 2007); ให้ก่อน surgery เสมอ — wait 2 wk for response
Macrocystic CPAM (dominant cyst): thoracoamniotic double-pigtail shunt → drain dominant cyst → ↓ mediastinal shift
Refractory + mirror syndrome: open resection at center of excellence
Postnatal: CT chest at 1 month; elective lobectomy by 6 months if symptomatic/significant
9. Fetal Anesthesia
Nociceptive pathways ≥~24–26 weeks; fetal anesthesia obligatory for all procedures ≥18–20 weeks — ethical standard + ↓ stress responses.

rows: ~60–70% maternal level reaches fetus; baseline for all open surgery
Fentanyl 10–20 mcg/kg + Vecuronium 0.2 mg/kg + Atropine 20 mcg/kg
Open surgery/EXIT — analgesia + immobility + vagal blockade

blocks.text:
Fetal Bradycardia During Open Surgery
Step 1: maternal hemodynamics (left tilt, fluid). Step 2: fetal IM atropine 20 mcg/kg. Step 3: intracardiac epinephrine. Step 4: emergency delivery + neonatal resus. Neonatologist + full resus equipment must be in OR throughout open fetal surgery.
10. Maternal Risks and Ethics
10.1 Maternal Risk Summary
10.2 Ethical Principles
Maternal autonomy paramount: full disclosure of maternal risks; fetal benefit cannot override maternal right to refuse
Equipoise: unproven procedures within RCT or outcomes registry only
NAFTNet + Eurofoetus registry: all centers enroll; generates ongoing evidence
Center of excellence: multidisciplinary team (MFM, pediatric surgery/neurosurgery, neonatology, anesthesia, ethics) + volume threshold
11. NICU Practical — Receiving the Post-Fetal Surgery Neonate
Review before delivery: operative report + contact fetal surgery team; know procedure + gestational age + anticipated problems
Post-MMC repair: PPROM + preterm expected; shunt still 40%; head US q2 wk; assess Chiari II (swallowing, vocal cord, lateral gaze); consults: neurosurgery, urology, ortho, ophthalmology
Post-TTTS laser: donor = anemia + IUGR; recipient = polycythemia (partial exchange if Hct >65%) + cardiomegaly; check TAPS by MCA Doppler; monitor cardiac function echo
Post-CDH FETO: NO bag-mask → immediate intubation + NGT; iNO; ECMO team on standby; repair after stabilization
EXIT handover: neonatologist scrubbed in OR; cord clamp only after CO2 confirmed; immediate full resus team
Post-LUTO shunt: own Cr from day 2–3; check shunt position; PUV ablation electively; long-term nephrology + urology
Any open fetal surgery: anticipate significant prematurity; antenatal steroids usually given; low surfactant threshold; MgSO4 if <32 wk
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — MOMS: 40% vs 82% Shunt — กดตัวเลขนี้
MOMS (Adzick NEJM 2011): prenatal MMC repair → shunt at 12M: 40% vs 82% (RR 0.48, p<0.001); motor function ~2 grades better. Maternal cost: 79% preterm, PPROM 46%, mandatory CS future. Criteria: T1–S1, 19–25+6 wk, BMI <35, singleton, no prior preterm. Fetoscopic (MOMS2) awaited.

PEARL 2 — TTTS: Laser coagulates anastomoses, ไม่ใช่แค่ drain fluid
Amniodrainage = temporary; laser = treat cause — coagulate all inter-twin anastomoses. Eurofetus: 76% vs 56% survival ≥1 twin (p=0.009). Solomon technique: equatorial line + selective → ↓ TAPS (3% vs 16%) + ↓ recurrence (1% vs 7%). TAPS post-laser = residual micro-anastomoses → MCA Doppler.

PEARL 3 — FETO: Severe CDH เท่านั้น, ไม่ใช่ Moderate
TOTAL: severe CDH benefit (40% vs 15%); moderate CDH NO benefit + ↑ PPROM 47%. ก่อน counsel ครอบครัว CDH: assess o/e LHR + liver position + MRI lung volume. Moderate → expectant + postnatal ECMO center. Severe isolated left CDH → specialized FETO center.

PEARL 4 — EXIT: Neonatologist Scrubbed In, ไม่ใช่รอนอก
EXIT: partial delivery on placenta → airway secured BEFORE cord clamp. Neonatologist: scrubbed + in OR before procedure; bronchoscopes 2.5+3.0 mm; paediatric surgeon in; tracheostomy tray; surfactant; ECMO notified; cord clamp ONLY after CO2 confirmed. ห้าม handover CHAOS infant to unprepared team.

PEARL 5 — Microcystic CPAM + Hydrops: Betamethasone ก่อน Surgery
Maternal betamethasone 12 mg IM × 2 doses q24h → regression ~75% microcystic CPAM with hydrops (Peranteau J Pediatr Surg 2007). ให้ก่อนพิจารณา surgery เสมอ — wait 2 wk for response. CVR >1.6 = 75% hydrops risk → ดู closely. ถ้า respond → ไม่ต้อง surgery. ถ้าไม่ respond + mirror syndrome → open resection.
13. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Harrison MR et al. NEJM 1981;306:591: First open fetal surgery (obstructive uropathy) at UCSF; proof of concept; launched fetal surgery field.
Senat MV et al. (Eurofetus). NEJM 2004;351:136: TTTS II–IV: fetoscopic laser vs amniodrainage; survival ≥1 twin 76% vs 56% (p=0.009); ↓ neurological morbidity. Established laser as standard of care.
Adzick NS et al. (MOMS). NEJM 2011;364:993: Open prenatal MMC repair: VP shunt 40% vs 82% (p<0.001); motor improvement ~2 grades. Stopped early for benefit. Mandatory center of excellence.
Morris RK et al. (PLUTO). Lancet 2013;382:1496: Vesicoamniotic shunting for LUTO: 31/150 enrolled; 50% vs 27% survival — underpowered (p=0.25). Best available evidence for LUTO.
Solomon C et al. (Solomon technique). Lancet 2014;383:503: Equatorial line + selective vs selective only: ↓ recurrent TTTS (1% vs 7%), ↓ TAPS (3% vs 16%). Solomon = preferred technique.
Lapa Neto DFC et al. Lancet 2018;392:1855: Fetoscopic vs open MMC repair: comparable neurological outcomes; ↓ preterm labor fetoscopic; informs MOMS2.
Deprest J et al. (TOTAL moderate). Lancet 2019;393:1512: FETO for moderate CDH: no benefit (54% vs 65%); PPROM 47% vs 11%. FETO not for moderate CDH.
Deprest J et al. (TOTAL severe). Lancet 2021;398:124: FETO for severe isolated left CDH: 40% vs 15% (p=0.008). FETO indicated at specialized centers.
Crombleholme TM et al. Radiology 2002;224:843: CVR >1.6 → 75% hydrops risk in CPAM; defines surveillance threshold and intervention indication.
Peranteau WH et al. J Pediatr Surg 2007;42:77: Maternal betamethasone for microcystic CPAM with hydrops: regression ~75%. First-line before surgical intervention.
Quintero RA et al. J Perinatol 1999;19:550: TTTS staging system I–V: universally adopted for management + prognosis.
Lee H et al. Am J Obstet Gynecol 2003;188:27: EXIT for CHAOS + giant neck masses: >90% airway success on placental support; validates EXIT as standard.
Hirose S et al. Semin Perinatol 2004;28:162: Tocolysis for open fetal surgery: indomethacin + MgSO4 combination; betamimetic → pulmonary edema risk. Basis for peri-operative tocolysis protocol.
Deprest J et al. Prenat Diagn 2004;24:1183: o/e LHR as CDH severity + prognosis indicator; defines FETO candidacy thresholds; validated by TOTAL trial.
14. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: MOMS trial criteria + primary outcome + maternal tradeoffs คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Criteria: singleton, MMC T1–S1, GA 19–25+6 wk, BMI <35, no chromosomal anomaly, no prior preterm, no uterine anomaly. Primary: death or VP shunt by 12M — 40% prenatal vs 82% postnatal (RR 0.48, p<0.001). Motor: ~2 grades better. Maternal cost: 79% born <37 wk, PPROM 46%, mandatory CS future pregnancies, pulmonary edema. Stopped early for benefit.
Q: TTTS Quintero stage II vs III ต่างกันอย่างไร? indications for laser คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Stage II: donor bladder absent on 60-min scan; Doppler normal. Stage III: abnormal Doppler — donor AEDF/REDF UA; recipient reversed a-wave DV or pulsatile UV. Both II and III → indications for fetoscopic laser (Eurofetus criteria). Stage I: ขึ้นกับ center (monitor vs laser). Stage IV: urgent laser.
Q: TOTAL trial: FETO benefit ใน severity ไหน? ทำไม moderate ไม่ได้ประโยชน์?

ตอบ: Severe CDH (o/e LHR <25%): FETO → survival 40% vs 15% (p=0.008) — significant benefit. Moderate CDH: NO benefit (54% vs 65%) + ↑ PPROM 47%. Reason: moderate CDH มี lung reserve + postnatal ECMO/management เพียงพอ → FETO risks (PPROM, preterm) ไม่คุ้ม. FETO = severe isolated left CDH only at specialized center.
Q: EXIT procedure คืออะไร? neonatologist ต้องเตรียมอะไรสำหรับ CHAOS?

ตอบ: EXIT = partial delivery on placental circulation → airway secured before cord clamp. Deep GA (uterine relaxation), hysterotomy, partial fetal exteriorization, airway procedure, cord clamp after airway confirmed. CHAOS: laryngeal/tracheal atresia → normal intubation impossible. Neonatologist: scrubbed + in OR; bronchoscopes 2.5+3.0 mm; paediatric surgeon scrubbed; tracheostomy tray; surfactant; ECMO notified; cord clamp ONLY after CO2.
Q: Fetal anesthesia ใน open surgery: agents + routes?

ตอบ: Two routes: (1) transplacental via maternal GA (halogenated agents) = baseline (~60–70% maternal level at fetus); (2) direct fetal IM: fentanyl 10–20 mcg/kg + vecuronium 0.2 mg/kg + atropine 20 mcg/kg → complete analgesia + immobility + vagal blockade. Fetal bradycardia: step 1 maternal hemodynamics → step 2 fetal IM atropine → step 3 intracardiac epi → step 4 emergency delivery.
Q: Microcystic CPAM with hydrops at 24 weeks, CVR 2.1 — first step?

ตอบ: Maternal betamethasone (12 mg IM × 2 doses q24h) — regression in ~75% microcystic CPAM with hydrops (Peranteau 2007). Give before any surgery — wait 2 wk for response. ถ้า respond → monitor. ถ้าไม่ respond + mirror syndrome → open resection. CVR >1.6 (= 2.1 here) → high risk for hydrops without intervention — intensive surveillance.
Q: PLUTO trial สำหรับ LUTO: ข้อสรุป + วิธี patient selection?

ตอบ: PLUTO: underpowered (31/150); 50% vs 27% survival — not significant (p=0.25); no definitive RCT evidence. Patient selection: serial bladder aspirations q48–72h × 3 (fresh urine) — favorable: Na <100 + Cl <90 + Osm <210 + β2-microglobulin <4 mg/L → preserved renal tubular function → more likely to benefit. Unfavorable markers → renal dysplasia likely → shunt ไม่ช่วย.
Q: Solomon technique ต่างจาก selective laser อย่างไร? ทำไมจึง preferred?

ตอบ: Selective: coagulate visualized anastomoses only. Solomon: selective + complete equatorial line across entire inter-twin membrane (Solomon Lancet 2014). ↓ recurrent TTTS (1% vs 7%) + ↓ TAPS (3% vs 16%) — microscopic anastomoses missed by selective are destroyed by equatorial line. Solomon = preferred standard technique.
Day 110 Preview

Fanaroff Ch13 — Adverse Exposures to the Fetus
Day 108 จะครอบคลุม Fanaroff Ch13: Adverse Exposures to the Fetus — teratogenesis mechanisms, critical periods, PLLR system (2015), radiation, environmental toxins. Topics: thalidomide, valproate, isotretinoin, warfarin embryopathy, FAS/FASD, cocaine, methamphetamine, radiation dose thresholds (≤50 mGy vs >100 mGy), methylmercury. Curriculum: Ch08 → Ch09 → Ch12 (today) → Ch13 next.
